욥기 40:2.content: “Shall he who argues contend with the Almighty? He who argues with God, let him answer it.”

욥기 40:4.content: “Behold, I am of small account. What will I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth.

욥기 40:5.content: I have spoken once, and I will not answer; Yes, twice, but I will proceed no further.”

욥기 40:6.content: Then Yahweh answered Job out of the whirlwind,

욥기 40:7.content: “Now brace yourself like a man. I will question you, and you will answer me.

욥기 40:8.content: Will you even annul my judgment? Will you condemn me, that you may be justified?

욥기 40:9.content: Or do you have an arm like God? Can you thunder with a voice like him?

욥기 40:10.content: “Now deck yourself with excellency and dignity. Array yourself with honor and majesty.

욥기 40:11.content: Pour out the fury of your anger. Look at everyone who is proud, and bring him low.

욥기 40:12.content: Look at everyone who is proud, and humble him. Crush the wicked in their place.

욥기 40:13.content: Hide them in the dust together. Bind their faces in the hidden place.

욥기 40:14.content: Then I will also admit to you that your own right hand can save you.

욥기 40:15.content: “See now, behemoth, which I made as well as you. He eats grass as an ox.

욥기 40:16.content: Look now, his strength is in his thighs. His force is in the muscles of his belly.

욥기 40:17.content: He moves his tail like a cedar. The sinews of his thighs are knit together.

욥기 40:18.content: His bones are like tubes of bronze. His limbs are like bars of iron.

욥기 40:19.content: He is the chief of the ways of God. He who made him gives him his sword.

욥기 40:20.content: Surely the mountains produce food for him, where all the animals of the field play.

욥기 40:21.content: He lies under the lotus trees, in the covert of the reed, and the marsh.

욥기 40:22.content: The lotuses cover him with their shade. The willows of the brook surround him.

욥기 40:23.content: Behold, if a river overflows, he doesn’t tremble. He is confident, though the Jordan swells even to his mouth.

욥기 40:24.content: Shall any take him when he is on the watch, or pierce through his nose with a snare?
